manifest.title: GoalOS Signoff Pro — Proof Before Settlement Institutional Research Lab v30
manifest.standard: No Proof. No Trust. No Settlement.

executiveTearSheet.title: GoalOS Signoff Pro — Proof Before Settlement Institutional Research Lab v30
executiveTearSheet.coreThesis: Blockchain proves the transaction. GoalOS proves the work.
executiveTearSheet.standard: No Proof. No Trust. No Settlement.
executiveTearSheet.targetDecision: Should a blockchain project be trusted, funded, upgraded, partnered with, or treated as settlement-ready?
executiveTearSheet.answer: Only after the proof package exists, passes review, and produces an explicit receipt-bound decision.
executiveActions: Require proof packages for material milestones.
Attach receipts to treasury, grant, audit, and upgrade decisions.
Use accepted proof as institutional memory, not raw marketing.

acceptancePredicate.expression: accept(mission) := mission.exists && evidence.complete && replay.available && validation.passed && risk.bounded && humanAuthority.explicit && receipt.signed && publicBoundary.disclosed
acceptancePredicate.plainEnglish: A mission is not settlement-ready merely because a result exists. It becomes settlement-ready only when the required proof, replay, validation, risk, authority, receipt, and boundary gates pass.

settlementSafetyInvariant.invariant: No accepted settlement signal without a complete proof package and explicit authority boundary.

claimMaturity.publicMeaning: A claim exists but no proof package has been submitted.
Evidence has been provided but not yet validated.
The project has run completeness and integrity checks.
The proof package is structured enough for external review.
A qualified independent party can reproduce or inspect the claim path.
Human/governance authority has accepted the claim within stated boundaries.
A signed receipt, public hash, or optional anchor records the decision.
The accepted proof may influence future missions, reputation, or reusable capability.

dueDiligenceRubric.scoring: 0 = missing, 1 = asserted, 2 = documented, 3 = replayable/reviewed, 4 = accepted with signed receipt

phases.name: Declare the standard
phases.output: Publicly state that important blockchain work requires mission, evidence, validation, receipt, and settlement-readiness boundaries.

phases.name: Require proof packages for new milestones
phases.output: DAO grants, treasury payments, audits, partnerships, and AI-agent tasks must define acceptance criteria before execution.

phases.name: Publish public-safe dockets
phases.output: Each material claim links to a redacted Evidence Docket, replay path, validator report, risk ledger, and human signoff.

phases.name: Bind receipts to governance and settlement readiness
phases.output: No payout, upgrade, milestone closure, or public credibility claim without an accepted Mission Receipt.

phases.name: Use proof as institutional memory
phases.output: Accepted work enters the Chronicle and influences future missions only with proof provenance.

adoptionProgram.primaryKpi: percentage of material claims linked to accepted proof packages
secondaryKpis: number of claims demoted due to missing proof

clauses.clause: No Proof Package, No Settlement Readiness
clauses.text: A milestone, grant, upgrade, audit remediation, AI-agent output, or treasury payment is not settlement-ready until the required proof package has been submitted, reviewed, and accepted.
Any material technical claim should provide a replay path, reproduction packet, or independent review route before it is used to justify governance, payment, or launch.
Automation may prepare evidence and recommend decisions, but high-impact acceptance remains bound to a named human or governance authority.
Every public proof package must state what it proves, what it does not prove, what remains unverified, and what risk remains.
Public demos and public dockets must not request personal/confidential data, use wallet integrations, move funds, or imply production settlement authority unless separately authorized.

mandatePack.modelRequirement: Any blockchain project seeking credibility should publish a public-safe proof package for material claims before requesting trust, funds, governance approval, or settlement readiness.

translation.stakeholderOutcome: everyone can ask where the proof is
raw claims cannot masquerade as accepted proof

translation.productSurface: No Proof / No Trust / No Settlement rule
translation.stakeholderOutcome: value moves only after proof qualifies
future work uses accepted proof, not noise

researchObjects.name: Work-Verification Gap
researchObjects.plain: A blockchain can settle a transaction without proving that the off-chain work behind it was real, complete, reviewed, or accepted.
researchObjects.institutionalUse: Makes the problem obvious to DAOs, auditors, grant programs, exchanges, partners, and users.

researchObjects.name: Proof Object Model
researchObjects.plain: Mission Contract, Evidence Docket, ProofBundle, Replay Log, Validator Report, Risk Ledger, Human Authority, Mission Receipt, Anchor, Chronicle.
researchObjects.institutionalUse: Gives teams a shared vocabulary for credible proof packages.

researchObjects.name: Acceptance Predicate
researchObjects.plain: Settlement readiness exists only when required proof objects exist, replay is possible, validation passes, risk is bounded, and the human authority gate is explicit.
researchObjects.institutionalUse: Turns vague milestone acceptance into a reviewable decision.

researchObjects.name: Settlement-Safety Invariant
researchObjects.plain: No proof package should produce settlement readiness unless its mission, evidence, replay, validation, risk, authority, receipt, and boundary gates pass.
researchObjects.institutionalUse: Creates a simple rule that stakeholders can demand.

researchObjects.name: Claim-Maturity Lattice
researchObjects.plain: Claims mature from unsubmitted to submitted, internally checked, independently replayed, accepted, anchored, challenged, and chronicle-admitted.
researchObjects.institutionalUse: Prevents raw claims from masquerading as institutional proof.

researchObjects.name: Proof Mandate
researchObjects.plain: Users, DAOs, investors, auditors, enterprises, and ecosystem partners can ask one question: where is the proof package?
researchObjects.institutionalUse: Converts the research into practical market pressure.

audienceQuestions.name: DAO Delegates
audienceQuestions.ask: Where is the proof package before we vote or release treasury funds?

audienceQuestions.name: Grant Committees
audienceQuestions.ask: Which evidence proves that the milestone was earned?

audienceQuestions.name: Auditors
audienceQuestions.ask: Can the remediation claim be replayed and reviewed independently?

audienceQuestions.name: Exchanges & Partners
audienceQuestions.ask: What evidence supports the project’s credibility claim?

audienceQuestions.name: Enterprises
audienceQuestions.ask: What acceptance record proves that this vendor delivery is complete?

audienceQuestions.name: Users
audienceQuestions.ask: Why should we trust this claim before value or governance power moves?
